title: Build first-class Pi ticks orchestrator extension
description: Implement the Pi runner as a Pi extension/package rather than relying on tmux. The extension should supervise parallel Pi subprocesses/SDK sessions for each ready tick, render wave status in Pi's TUI, expose /ticks-run /ticks-plan /ticks-status, and preserve runner-neutral durable state via git worktrees, branches, reports, and .tick files. The distributable skill can include the adapter docs and prompts, but executable UX should live as an extension bundled in a Pi package or project .pi/extensions path.
notes:
2026-06-16 09:00 - Plannotator feedback incorporated into pi-runner.md: prefer repo-tracked .tick/config.md Pi Orchestrator section for model/vendor routing, and clarify that skills cannot directly activate extensions under current Pi model; distribute as a Pi package bundling skills + extensions.
2026-07-09 13:15 - Planned from docs/projects/2026-07-09-pi-orchestrator-extension/SPEC.md. Scope emphasizes a purpose-built Pi control-tower extension: /ticks-plan, /ticks-run, /ticks-status, /ticks-dashboard; worktree/process supervision; rich status; verification/merge/recovery; package distribution with the ticks skill.
acceptance_criteria:
- A Pi package manifest loads both the ticks skill and orchestrator extension.
- /ticks-run, /ticks-plan, /ticks-status, and /ticks-dashboard provide useful dry-run/status behavior.
- /ticks-run <epic> --dry-run shows wave, tick, worktree, and model plans without agents.
- Worktree execution runs a disposable tick through child Pi supervision, artifacts, boundary check, merge, close, and cleanup.
- Dashboard shows wave progress, agent cards, verifier/merge lanes, cost/context telemetry, and recovery state.
- Documentation covers installation, commands, configuration, durable state, recovery, and limitations.
